name: Database Schema
description: Ecto migrations and schemas for all database tables with indexes and foreign key constraints
specification:
## Overview
Create complete database schema for Lex based on SPEC.md section 4 (Domain Model), plus additional user tables for local-only authentication.

## Scope
This feature creates:
1. Ecto migrations (one per table) in dependency order
2. Ecto schema modules for each table
3. Indexes for all foreign keys and common query patterns
4. Foreign key constraints with proper on_delete/on_update behavior

## Tables (12 total)
1. `users` - Local user accounts (name, email, primary_language)
2. `user_target_languages` - User's target languages for learning
3. `documents` - Uploaded EPUBs with processing status
4. `sections` - Chapters/logical blocks within documents
5. `sentences` - Individual sentences within sections
6. `lexemes` - Lemma-level vocabulary entries (language-scoped)
7. `tokens` - Token instances within sentences
8. `user_lexeme_states` - User's vocabulary state per lexeme
9. `user_sentence_states` - User's read/unread state per sentence
10. `reading_positions` - User's current position in each document
11. `llm_help_requests` - Log of LLM assistance requests
12. `reading_events` - Audit log of reader interactions

## Out of Scope
- Authentication flows (local-only, no password reset, etc.)
- Database seeding scripts
- Query optimization beyond indexes

## Technical Constraints
- SQLite via ecto_sqlite3
- Hard deletes only (no deleted_at columns)
- Foreign key constraints enforced
- Migrations in dependency order

## Definition of Done
- All 12 migrations created and runnable
- All 12 Ecto schema modules created
- Indexes on all foreign keys
- Indexes on common query patterns (user_id lookups, unique constraints)
- Foreign key constraints with appropriate on_delete behavior
- Migrations can be run successfully (mix ecto.migrate)

name: EPUB Import Orchestration
description: Build end-to-end import pipeline that coordinates EPUB parsing, NLP processing, and database creation
specification:
## Goal
Provide a single function to import an EPUB file into the Lex database, creating all necessary records with transactional safety.

## Technical Approach
Create `Lex.Library.import_epub/2` that orchestrates:
1. Parse EPUB metadata → create Document (status: "uploaded")
2. Extract chapters → create Sections
3. For each chapter: extract text → NLP process → create Sentences and Tokens
4. Link Tokens to Lexemes (create if needed)
5. Update Document status to "ready"

## Database Transaction
Wrap entire import in `Repo.transaction()` so any failure rolls back all changes. No partial commits allowed.

## Interface
```elixir
Lex.Library.import_epub(file_path, opts \\ [])
# Options:
#   :user_id - required, associate with this user
#   :source_file - override source file path (default: file_path)
# => {:ok, %Document{}} | {:error, reason}
```

## Key Decisions
- Create Lexemes with unique constraint on [:language, :normalized_lemma, :pos]
- Skip chapters with no text content
- Progress logging via optional callback function
- If NLP fails on one chapter, entire transaction rolls back

## Error Handling
- `{:error, {:epub_parse_failed, reason}}` - EPUB parsing error
- `{:error, {:nlp_failed, section_title, reason}}` - NLP processing error
- `{:error, {:validation_failed, changeset}}` - Database validation error
- `{:error, {:transaction_failed, reason}}` - Transaction rollback

## Files to Create
- `lib/lex/library/import.ex` - orchestration module
- May need to add functions to existing contexts (Library, Text)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Successfully imports El Principito with all chapters
- [ ] Creates Document, Sections, Sentences, Tokens, Lexemes
- [ ] Document status transitions: uploaded → ready
- [ ] Transaction rolls back on any failure (no partial data)
- [ ] Integration test with test EPUB fixture
- [ ] Handles EPUB with missing metadata gracefully

name: EPUB Parsing Foundation
description: Add dependencies and modules to parse EPUB metadata and extract chapter content
specification:
## Goal
Enable Lex to parse EPUB files to extract:
- Metadata: title, author, language
- Chapter list with positions
- Raw text content from each chapter

## Technical Approach
Add `paper_street` (EPUB parser) and `SweetXml` (XML utilities) dependencies. Create `Lex.Library.EPUB` module with:

1. `parse_metadata(path)` - Returns map with :title, :author, :language from OPF metadata
2. `list_chapters(path)` - Returns ordered list of chapters [{:id, :title, :href}, ...]
3. `get_chapter_content(path, href)` - Returns plain text (strip HTML tags)

## Key Decisions
- Filter out front matter and back matter by excluding items that aren't in `spine` element or have non-linear="yes"
- Chapter order follows the spine, not the manifest
- Plain text extraction: strip HTML tags but preserve paragraph breaks as newlines
- Language code comes from OPF metadata `dc:language` element

## Error Handling
- Return `{:ok, result}` or `{:error, reason}` tuples
- Handle malformed EPUBs gracefully with descriptive errors

## Files to Create/Modify
- `mix.exs` - add dependencies
- `lib/lex/library/epub.ex` - new module

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Can parse El Principito EPUB and extract correct title, author, language
- [ ] Returns ordered list of chapters (excluding front/back matter)
- [ ] Can extract readable text from any chapter
- [ ] Returns proper error tuples for malformed EPUBs
- [ ] Unit tests with test EPUB fixture

name: LLM Session Connection Reuse
description: Reduce LLM TTFT by reusing a persistent OpenAI-compatible connection per LiveView session
specification:
## Goal
Reduce LLM first-token latency (TTFT) as much as possible by reusing a persistent OpenAI-compatible HTTP connection for repeated token-help requests within a single Reader LiveView session.

## Non-Goals
- No model/provider prompt changes
- No multi-provider support beyond OpenAI-compatible chat completions
- No hard performance benchmark gate in CI
- No schema migration for TTFT persistence (logs-only telemetry)

## User-Visible Behavior
- Repeated help requests in one reader session should feel faster to start streaming.
- If the persistent connection drops, requests continue seamlessly via automatic reconnect and retry.
- Existing reader behavior (popup, streaming, dismiss, caching, errors) remains unchanged.

## Technical Constraints
- Scope is per LiveView session (not app-global pooling).
- Endpoint remains OpenAI-compatible `/chat/completions` SSE streaming.
- Reuse transport connection across requests where possible.
- Retry must be transparent for recoverable connection-drop cases.
- Keep TTFT instrumentation in logs only.

## Definition of Done
- Reader LiveView has a per-session LLM transport owner/lifecycle.
- LLM client path reuses existing connection between requests in same session.
- Recoverable dropped/stale connection is automatically re-established and request proceeds.
- TTFT logs clearly indicate first-token latency for requests and can distinguish reused vs reconnected paths.
- Automated tests cover lifecycle, reuse, reconnect fallback, and no-regression streaming behavior.

name: LLM Streaming Help Fixups
description: Close implementation gaps for dismiss event handling, timeout consistency, and latency persistence
specification:
## Goal
Address the three identified implementation gaps in Reader LLM help so behavior matches product requirements and metadata is reliably persisted.

## In Scope
1. Wire and handle `dismiss_llm_popup` LiveView event.
2. Enforce consistent 5-second timeout behavior for streaming requests.
3. Persist accurate `latency_ms` for LLM help requests.

## Out of Scope
- Request cancellation semantics (dismiss remains UI-only)
- Prompt/content changes
- New provider integrations
- Visual redesign of popup

## User-Visible Behavior
- Clicking popup close button or pressing space while popup is open reliably hides the popup.
- LLM requests that exceed 5 seconds surface timeout error promptly.
- No direct UI change for latency, but request records include realistic latency values.

## Definition of Done
- All three gaps are fixed and covered by automated tests.
- Existing LLM help behavior (streaming, caching, error display) remains intact.
- New tests pass with existing suite for reader/vocab/llm modules.

name: LLM Streaming Help in Reader
description: Add LLM-powered help popup in reader UI triggered by space key, with streaming responses stored in database
specification:
## Feature Overview
When a user is reading and presses space on a highlighted word, a popup appears showing streaming LLM help explaining the word meaning, lexeme, and usage in context. The response is stored in the database for caching. Pressing space again dismisses the popup.

## User Flow
1. User navigates to a word using w/b keys (visual highlighting)
2. User presses space to trigger help
3. Popup appears immediately (right gutter on desktop, below text on mobile)
4. LLM response streams in via WebSocket
5. User presses space again to dismiss
6. Full response saved to llm_help_requests table
7. User continues reading

## Technical Requirements
- OpenAI-style chat completions API support (configurable provider/endpoint)
- 5-second timeout with error message
- Response caching by (sentence_id, token_id, response_language)
- Streaming via Phoenix LiveView push_event to JavaScript
- Config via config/runtime.exs with LLM_API_KEY from env
- Mock LLM for testing

## Data Model
Uses existing `llm_help_requests` table with columns:
- user_id, document_id, sentence_id, token_id
- request_type ("token" or "sentence")
- response_language (user's primary language)
- provider, model, latency_ms
- prompt_tokens, completion_tokens
- response_text

## Prompt Context
- Target word (token surface form + lexeme info)
- Sentence text (full context)
- Document title and author
- User's primary language (so LLM responds in correct language)

## UI/UX
- Popup renders to right of reader text on desktop (gutter)
- Popup appears below text on mobile (flex layout)
- Streaming text appears as it arrives
- Dismissible with space key

name: NLP Integration Layer
description: Create module to coordinate with Python NLP pipeline for text processing
specification:
## Goal
Provide Elixir interface to the existing Python NLP pipeline at `priv/python/lex_nlp.py`.

## Technical Approach
Create `Lex.Text.NLP` module that:
1. Writes text to temp file
2. Calls `python priv/python/lex_nlp.py --input <temp_file> --output <output_file>` via `System.cmd/3`
3. Reads and parses JSON output
4. Returns structured data for Sentence/Token creation

## Interface
```elixir
Lex.Text.NLP.process_text(text, opts \\ [])
# => {:ok, [%{position: 1, text: "...", tokens: [%{position: 1, surface: "...", ...}]}
```

## Key Decisions
- Use temp files rather than stdin/stdout to avoid encoding issues
- 30-second timeout for Python process
- Clean up temp files in `ensure` block
- Validate JSON structure before returning

## Error Handling
- `{:error, :python_not_found}` - Python not in PATH
- `{:error, {:python_exit, code, stderr}}` - Python crashed
- `{:error, {:invalid_json, reason}}` - Bad JSON output
- `{:error, {:model_missing, msg}}` - spaCy model not installed

## Files to Create
- `lib/lex/text/nlp.ex` - new module

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Successfully processes Spanish text through Python pipeline
- [ ] Returns list of sentences with token data
- [ ] Proper error handling for all failure modes
- [ ] Cleans up temp files even on failure
- [ ] Unit tests with mocked System.cmd

name: Python NLP Pipeline with spaCy
description: Implement spaCy-based NLP pipeline for Spanish text processing with JSON output matching database schemas
specification:
## Goal
Replace the existing Stanza-based skeleton with a production-ready spaCy NLP pipeline that processes Spanish text and outputs JSON matching the Elixir database schemas.

## Context
- **Library**: spaCy with `es_core_news_md` model
- **Input**: Raw text files (UTF-8 encoded)
- **Output**: JSON with sentences and tokens structure
- **Caller**: Elixir app will invoke via CLI per text section
- **Entry point**: Flat script at `priv/python/lex_nlp.py`

## JSON Output Contract
Output must match Ecto schema requirements:

```json
{
  "sentences": [
    {
      "position": 1,
      "text": "Hola mundo.",
      "char_start": 0,
      "char_end": 11,
      "tokens": [
        {
          "position": 1,
          "surface": "Hola",
          "normalized_surface": "hola",
          "lemma": "hola",
          "pos": "INTJ",
          "is_punctuation": false,
          "char_start": 0,
          "char_end": 4
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

## Field Mappings (spaCy → JSON)
- `token.text` → `surface`
- `token.text.lower()` → `normalized_surface`  
- `token.lemma_` → `lemma`
- `token.pos_` → `pos`
- `token.is_punct` → `is_punctuation`
- `token.idx` / `token.idx + len(token)` → `char_start` / `char_end`
- `sent.text` → `text`
- `sent.start_char` / `sent.end_char` → sentence `char_start` / `char_end`

## Technical Requirements
- Model loaded once and cached (singleton pattern or module-level)
- UTF-8 encoding for all text I/O
- Exit code 0 on success, non-zero on error
- Error messages written to stderr
- Positions start at 1 (not 0) per database schema

## Files to Modify/Create
1. `priv/python/pyproject.toml` - update dependencies
2. `priv/python/lex_nlp.py` - new flat script entry point
3. `priv/python/lex_nlp/nlp_pipeline.py` - spaCy implementation
4. `priv/python/tests/test_pipeline.py` - smoke test

## Dependencies
- `spacy>=3.7.0`
- `es-core-news-md` model (manual install or documented)

name: Reader Core
description: Build the core reading interface with sentence navigation, token focus, and position persistence
specification:
## Goal
Deliver a functional sentence-focused reader interface where users can navigate documents with keyboard shortcuts, see vocabulary state visualized as color-coded boxes, and resume reading where they left off.

## Scope
- Document listing page with progress indicators
- Single-sentence reader view with large typography
- Keyboard navigation (j/k for sentences, w/b for tokens)
- Token status visualization (color boxes for new/seen/learning)
- Reading position persistence (sentence-level)
- Skip section functionality

## Out of Scope (Milestone 4/5)
- State transitions (seen→known on advance)
- LLM help integration and learning trigger
- Reading events logging
- Progress tracking (% complete calculation will be stubbed or simplified)

## Technical Approach
- LiveView for all UI
- Minimal JS hook for keyboard capture
- Reader context for position management
- Components for token rendering with CSS classes
- URL-based navigation: /library, /read/:document_id

## Key Decisions
- Color boxes for token status (not underlines)
- Known words have default styling (no box)
- Sentence-level position persistence only
- Immediate skip (no confirmation modal)
- % complete shown in document listing

name: Reader UI Navigation Improvements
description: Improve reader page navigation with library button, dismiss LLM popup on navigation, and fix space key behavior for word status toggle
specification:
## Feature Overview
Improve the reader page UX with better navigation and refined keyboard interactions.

## Requirements

### 1. Library Navigation Button
- Add a "Library" button in the top-left of the reader page
- Button should match the styling of the existing "Skip Section" button
- Counters (R/L/K) should be positioned to the right of the Library button
- Final layout: `[Library Button] [R/L/K Counters] [Title/Author] [Skip Section]`
- Clicking navigates to `/library` route

### 2. Navigation Dismisses LLM Popup
- All navigation keybinds should first dismiss any open LLM popup
- Affected keys: `j`, `k`, `w`, `b`, and arrow keys (if supported)
- Navigation should still occur after dismissal
- This prevents the popup from staying open while reading moves to a new sentence

### 3. Space Key Behavior (Word Status Toggle)
**Current Behavior:**
- Space (popup closed) → opens popup, sets word to learning
- Space (popup open) → closes popup, word stays learning
- Space again → opens popup, sets word to known

**Desired Behavior:**
- Space (popup closed) → sets word to learning AND opens LLM popup
- Space (popup open) → sets word to known AND closes LLM popup

This creates a proper two-state toggle: popup closed = learning state, popup open = known state.

## Technical Context
- Reader LiveView: `lib/lex_web/live/reader_live/show.ex`
- Template: `lib/lex_web/live/reader_live/show.html.heex`
- Key navigation handled in `handle_event("key_nav", ...)`
- LLM popup state: `llm_popup_visible` assign
- Word learning toggle: `Vocab.toggle_learning/2` or `Vocab.advance_help_state/2`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Library button visible and styled consistently with Skip Section button
- [ ] Library button navigates to `/library` on click
- [ ] Counter layout updated with Library button on left
- [ ] All navigation keys dismiss LLM popup when open
- [ ] Space key properly toggles word: closed→learning+open, open→known+close
- [ ] All changes have comprehensive LiveView tests

name: Reader progress bars
description: Add section progress bar in header and document progress bar in footer with matching font color
specification:
## Goal
Add visual progress indicators to the reader view to show reading progress at both section and document levels.

## Requirements

### Section Progress Bar (Header)
- Display a progress bar showing current position within the current section
- Located in the header area near the section breadcrumb
- Color: match the font color (text-slate-300 or similar)

### Document Progress Bar (Footer)
- Display a progress bar showing overall progress through the entire document
- Located in the footer area with controls
- Color: match the font color (text-slate-300 or similar)

### Technical Implementation
1. Add functions to Reader context:
   - `get_section_progress(section_id, sentence_id)` - returns percentage 0-100
   - `get_document_progress(document_id, sentence_id)` - returns percentage 0-100
   
2. Update ReaderLive.Show:
   - Load progress data in mount
   - Update progress on navigation
   
3. Update template:
   - Add section progress bar in header
   - Add document progress bar in footer
   - Style bars with matching font color

### Visual Design
- Progress bars should be thin (2-4px height)
- Use the same color as the surrounding text
- Smooth transitions when progress updates
- Optional: show percentage text

name: Vocabulary State Engine
description: Implement automatic vocabulary state transitions, learning actions, and reading event logging
specification:
## Goal
Implement the complete vocabulary state machine with automatic transitions (seen→known), explicit learning actions, and comprehensive event logging.

## Scope

### State Transitions (per SPEC §5.1)
1. **First encounter**: Non-punctuation lexemes become `seen` on first view
2. **Advance (j key)**: `seen → known` for non-learning lexemes in current sentence
3. **Advance protection**: `learning` words stay `learning`
4. **Known permanence**: `known` never auto-demotes
5. **Backward safe**: `k` key never changes state
6. **Skip safe**: Skip actions never change state

### Sentence State (per SPEC §5.2)
- Sentence becomes `read` when advancing via normal next action
- Skipped sentences remain `unread`

### Learning Actions
- Toggle `learning` state on focused token
- LLM help (spacebar) automatically marks focused token as `learning`

### Event Logging (per SPEC §4.2 `reading_events`)
Log all reader interactions:
- `enter_sentence` - When sentence is displayed
- `advance_sentence` - When j is pressed
- `skip_range` - When skip section used
- `mark_learning` - When word marked learning
- `unmark_learning` - When learning removed
- `llm_help_requested` - When spacebar pressed

## Out of Scope
- UI for viewing learning list / vocabulary dashboard (M5)
- LLM provider integration (M5 - just logging the request here)
- Performance optimization

## Technical Approach
- Extend `Lex.Reader` context with state transition functions
- Wrap all state changes in transactions
- Batch lexeme updates per sentence to minimize DB writes
- Event logging is fire-and-forget (don't fail if logging fails)

## Key Decisions
- Transitions happen on `advance_sentence` (j key), not on enter
- Reading events logged asynchronously when possible
- LLM help request logging includes token_id and sentence_id

name: calibre-library-mirror
description: Display Calibre library folder as unified view with metadata and async import
specification:
## Feature: Calibre Library Mirror

### Goal
Display the user's Calibre library folder (`~/CalibreLibrary`) as a unified view on the `/library` page, showing all EPUB files with their metadata, import status, and ability to import asynchronously.

### User Visible Behavior
1. **Unified Library View**: The `/library` page shows ALL EPUB files from the Calibre folder
2. **Metadata Display**: Each book shows title, author parsed from EPUB metadata
3. **Import Status Indicators**:
   - Unimported: Shows "Import" button
   - Importing: Shows spinner/progress indicator
   - Imported: Shows "Imported" badge, no import button
   - Failed: Shows error message inline
4. **Async Import**: User can click "Import" on multiple books simultaneously
5. **Manual Refresh**: "Refresh" button to rescan Calibre folder

### Technical Requirements
- Calibre path configurable via `CALIBRE_LIBRARY_PATH` env var (defaults to `~/CalibreLibrary`)
- Path expansion for `~` home directory
- Efficient duplicate detection by matching `source_file` in Documents table
- Async import using Elixir Tasks with state tracking
- Real-time UI updates via PubSub or message passing

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All EPUB files from Calibre folder appear on /library page
- [ ] Each book displays parsed metadata (title, author)
- [ ] Already-imported books show "Imported" badge and no import button
- [ ] Clicking "Import" starts async process without blocking UI
- [ ] Multiple imports can run simultaneously
- [ ] Import progress shown with spinner
- [ ] Import errors displayed inline
- [ ] "Refresh" button rescans folder and updates view
- [ ] All code has tests

### Non-Goals
- No automatic background scanning (manual refresh only)
- No re-import functionality for already-imported books
- No file system watching/inotify
- No bulk import (import one at a time, but can queue multiple)

name: dark-theme-reading
description: Dark theme with serif font for eye-friendly extended reading
specification:

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dark background with proper contrast ratios (WCAG AA)
- [ ] Merriweather font applied globally
- [ ] Reading view centered in viewport
- [ ] Three-sentence display with clear visual hierarchy

name: misc

specification: Use this feature in cases where a task is minimal and does not require a feature, such as minor hotfixes, tweaks etc.

name: target-language-filtered-calibre-imports
description: Filter importable Calibre books by user target languages with blocking profile setup.
specification: Implement importable-book filtering in Lex so users only see Calibre/EPUB books whose EPUB metadata language matches one of their configured target languages (ISO code based), while still showing unknown-language books with a red "Language Unknown" flag. Add a blocking Library-page profile modal for the default startup user to require valid name, valid email (existing user changeset validation), and at least one target language before normal use. Use `user_target_languages` as the source of configured targets. EPUB metadata language is authoritative for matching. Matching should treat regional tags as base-language matches (e.g., es-ES and es-MX match target es). Scope applies to importable list only; existing imported-book behavior is unchanged. Include backend and LiveView test coverage for language extraction/normalization, filtering rules, unknown-language display, zero-target behavior, and blocking modal behavior.

name: Add CSS styles for Library page components
description: Create CSS styles for library-container, library-header, library-grid, library-item, and related component classes to match dark theme design
specification:
## Task: Add CSS Styles for Library Page Components

### Goal
Add comprehensive CSS styling for the Library page components to match the dark theme design and provide a polished, usable interface.

### Current State
The Library page (`lib/lex_web/live/library_live/index.html.heex`) uses these custom class names that have NO corresponding CSS rules:
- `.library-container`
- `.library-header`
- `.refresh-btn`
- `.calibre-notice`
- `.empty-state`
- `.sub-text`
- `.library-grid`
- `.library-item` (with modifiers: `.calibre`, `.imported`, `.importing`, `.error`)
- `.item-content`
- `.item-title`
- `.item-author`
- `.source-badge`
- `.item-actions`
- `.import-btn`
- `.importing-indicator`
- `.imported-badge`
- `.progress-container`, `.progress-bar`, `.progress-fill`, `.progress-text`
- `.read-btn`
- `.retry`
- `.error-message`

### Requirements

#### Layout & Container
- Library container: max-width, centered, padding, dark background matching theme
- Header: flex layout with title left, refresh button right
- Grid: responsive CSS grid for book cards (1 column mobile, 2 tablet, 3+ desktop)

#### Cards (library-item)
- Dark card background (`bg-slate-800` or custom dark surface)
- Rounded corners, subtle border
- Hover state with slight elevation
- Source badge positioning (top-right corner)

#### Import Status Visual States
- `.not_imported`: Standard card with "Import" button visible
- `.importing`: Spinner animation, disabled state, "Importing..." text
- `.imported`: "Imported" badge, "Read" button (not Import)
- `.error`: Red tint/border, error message text, "Retry" button

#### Buttons
- Import button: Primary action style (accent color)
- Read button: Secondary style
- Refresh button: Outline/subtle style
- Hover states for all buttons

#### Typography
- Title: Large, readable, proper line height
- Author: Muted color, smaller
- Empty state: Centered, helpful messaging

#### Progress Bar
- Container: Background track
- Fill: Accent color showing percentage
- Text: Percentage label

### Implementation Approach
Add styles to `assets/css/app.css` using `@layer components`:

```css
@layer components {
  .library-container { /* ... */ }
  .library-header { /* ... */ }
  /* etc */
}
```

name: Add LLM help context functions
description: Add cache lookup, prompt building, and orchestration functions to Lex.Vocab context
specification:
## Task: Add LLM Help Context Functions

Extend `Lex.Vocab` context with functions for LLM help request handling including caching.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Cache Lookup Function**
   - `get_cached_llm_response(sentence_id, token_id, response_language)`
   - Returns `{:ok, LlmHelpRequest.t()}` if found
   - Returns `{:error, :not_found}` if no cached response
   - Query llm_help_requests table where response_text IS NOT NULL

2. **Prompt Builder Function**
   - `build_llm_prompt(token, sentence, document, user)`
   - Takes Token struct, Sentence struct, Document struct, User struct
   - Returns system message and user message for chat completion
   - System prompt: "You are a helpful language learning assistant. Explain words briefly."
   - User prompt includes:
     - Word: token.surface (lemma: token.lemma, pos: token.pos)
     - Sentence context: sentence.text
     - Document: title and author
     - Response language instruction: "Respond in #{user.primary_language}"

3. **Request Orchestration Function**
   - `request_llm_help(user_id, document_id, sentence_id, token_id, stream_callback)`
   - First checks cache, returns cached response if available
   - If not cached:
     - Creates LlmHelpRequest record with pending state (response_text: nil)
     - Builds prompt
     - Calls LLM.Client.stream_chat_completion/2 with callback
     - On completion, updates LlmHelpRequest with response_text, latency_ms, token counts
   - Returns `{:ok, request_id}` immediately (streaming continues in background)
   - Returns `{:error, reason}` on immediate failures (not configured, etc.)

4. **Request Record Update Function**
   - `finalize_llm_request(request_id, response_text, latency_ms, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens)`
   - Updates the LlmHelpRequest record with full response data
   - Called when streaming completes

## Files to Modify
- `lib/lex/vocab.ex`

## Testing
- Unit tests for cache lookup
- Unit tests for prompt building
- Integration tests with mocked LLM client
- Tests for cache hit vs miss scenarios
completion_summary:
Successfully implemented all required LLM help context functions in Lex.Vocab:

1. **get_cached_llm_response/3** - Cache lookup function that queries llm_help_requests table for existing responses by sentence_id, token_id, and response_language. Returns {:ok, LlmHelpRequest.t()} if found with response_text IS NOT NULL, otherwise {:error, :not_found}.

2. **build_llm_prompt/4** - Prompt builder function that takes Token, Sentence, Document, and User structs and returns {system_message, user_message} tuple with proper context for the LLM.

3. **request_llm_help/5** - Orchestration function that:
   - Checks cache first and returns cached response if available
   - Creates LlmHelpRequest record with pending state
   - Builds prompts and calls Lex.LLM.Client.stream_chat_completion/2
   - Returns {:ok, request_id} immediately
   - Handles {:error, :not_configured} when LLM is not configured
   - Supports custom stream callbacks for UI updates

4. **finalize_llm_request/5** - Update function that records the full response text, latency_ms, and token counts when streaming completes.

All functions include comprehensive tests covering:
- Cache hit and miss scenarios
- Response language filtering
- Multiple cached responses
- Prompt building with different user languages
- Request finalization with full and partial data
- Error cases (user not found, token not found, LLM not configured)

Code passes all quality checks:
- mix compile: No warnings
- mix format --check-formatted: Pass
- mix test test/lex/vocab_test.exs: 55 tests, 0 failures
- Full test suite: 297 tests, 1 pre-existing failure unrelated to changes

## Task: Add LLM Runtime Configuration

   - Store in `config :lex, :llm_api_key`

2. **Provider Configuration**
   - Read `LLM_PROVIDER` from environment variable (optional, default: "openai")
   - Store in `config :lex, :llm_provider`

3. **Model Configuration**
   - Read `LLM_MODEL` from environment variable (optional, default: "gpt-4o-mini")
   - Store in `config :lex, :llm_model`

4. **Base URL Configuration**
   - Read `LLM_BASE_URL` from environment variable (optional, default: "https://api.openai.com/v1")
   - Store in `config :lex, :llm_base_url`
   - This allows custom providers that use OpenAI-compatible API

5. **Timeout Configuration**
   - Read `LLM_TIMEOUT_MS` from environment variable (optional, default: 5000)
   - Store in `config :lex, :llm_timeout_ms`

name: Add edge case tests for state transitions
description: Comprehensive tests for all vocabulary state edge cases
specification:
## Goal
Ensure all edge cases from SPEC §12 are covered by tests.

## Required Edge Case Tests

### Skip Content Behavior
- [ ] Skip section does not mark words `known`
- [ ] Skip section does not mark sentences `read`
- [ ] Skip to end of document works

### Advance Behavior  
- [ ] Advancing sentence promotes non-learning words to `known`
- [ ] Learning words stay `learning` on advance
- [ ] Known words stay `known` on advance
- [ ] Punctuation tokens ignored for progression
- [ ] Sentence becomes `read` only on normal advance

### First Encounter
- [ ] First view marks all non-punct lexemes `seen`
- [ ] Repeated tokens in one sentence don't create duplicate state rows
- [ ] Second view of same sentence doesn't increment seen_count twice

### Backward Navigation
- [ ] Going backward (k) never changes lexeme state
- [ ] Going backward never marks sentence read

### Learning Actions
- [ ] Toggle learning creates state if none exists
- [ ] Unmarking learning reverts to seen
- [ ] Known words cannot be unmarked
- [ ] LLM help marks word learning

### State Permanence
- [ ] Known words never demote automatically
- [ ] Learning only changes via explicit action

## Test Organization
Create dedicated test file for edge cases:
`test/lex/vocab/state_transitions_test.exs`

## Files
- New test file for comprehensive edge case coverage
completion_summary:
Successfully implemented comprehensive edge case tests for vocabulary state transitions. The test file covers all edge cases from SPEC §12:

- Skip Content Behavior (3 tests): Skip section doesn't mark words as known or sentences as read, skip to end of document works
- Advance Behavior (5 tests): Non-learning words promoted to known on advance, learning/known words stay in their state, punctuation ignored for progression, sentence marked read only on normal advance
- First Encounter (3 tests): Non-punct lexemes marked as seen on first view, repeated tokens don't create duplicates, second view doesn't increment seen_count
- Backward Navigation (2 tests): Going backward never changes lexeme state or marks sentences read
- Learning Actions (4 tests): Toggle learning creates state if needed, unmarking reverts to seen, known words cannot be unmarked, LLM help marks word as learning
- State Permanence (2 tests): Known words never demote automatically, learning only changes via explicit action

All 19 tests pass, code is properly formatted, and passes all strict credo checks.

name: Add progress calculation functions to Reader context
description: Add get_section_progress and get_document_progress functions to calculate reading progress percentages
specification:
Add two new public functions to `lib/lex/reader.ex`:

1. `get_section_progress(section_id, sentence_id)` 
   - Returns `{:ok, percentage}` where percentage is 0-100 float
   - Calculate by finding current sentence's position within section and total sentences in section
   - Formula: (current_sentence_position / total_sentences) * 100

2. `get_document_progress(document_id, sentence_id)`
   - Returns `{:ok, percentage}` where percentage is 0-100 float
   - Calculate by counting all sentences up to and including current sentence across all sections
   - Formula: (sentences_read / total_document_sentences) * 100

Both functions should:
- Handle edge cases (empty sections, single sentence, etc.)
- Use Ecto queries with Repo.aggregate for counting
- Return 0.0 if no progress possible
completion_summary:
Added two new functions to Reader context:

1. `get_section_progress/2` - Calculates percentage 0-100 of sentences read within a section
2. `get_document_progress/2` - Calculates percentage 0-100 of sentences read within the entire document

Both functions:
- Return {:ok, float} with percentage rounded to 1 decimal place
- Handle edge cases (empty sections, nil sentences)
- Use Ecto queries with Repo.aggregate for counting
- Consider sentence positions for accurate progress calculation

name: Add pytest smoke test
description: Create minimal smoke test verifying pipeline produces valid JSON structure
specification:
## Goal
Add a minimal pytest test that verifies the NLP pipeline works end-to-end.

## Scope
Create `priv/python/tests/test_pipeline.py`:

### Test Case: Basic Spanish Text
Test with input: `"Hola mundo. ¿Cómo estás?"`

Expected output structure:
```json
{
  "sentences": [
    {
      "position": 1,
      "text": "Hola mundo.",
      "char_start": 0,
      "char_end": 11,
      "tokens": [
        {"position": 1, "surface": "Hola", ...},
        {"position": 2, "surface": "mundo", ...},
        {"position": 3, "surface": ".", ..., "is_punctuation": true}
      ]
    },
    {
      "position": 2,
      "text": "¿Cómo estás?",
      "char_start": 12,
      "char_end": 24,
      "tokens": [...]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Assertions
1. Output has `"sentences"` key with list value
2. At least 2 sentences (split on period)
3. Each sentence has required fields: `position`, `text`, `char_start`, `char_end`, `tokens`
4. Sentence positions are 1-indexed and sequential
5. Each token has required fields: `position`, `surface`, `normalized_surface`, `lemma`, `pos`, `is_punctuation`, `char_start`, `char_end`
6. Token positions within sentence are 1-indexed and sequential
7. `is_punctuation` is True for "." and "¿"
8. Character offsets are consistent (non-overlapping, within sentence bounds)

### Test Execution
Use `NLPPipeline` directly (not CLI) for faster testing:
```python
from lex_nlp.nlp_pipeline import NLPPipeline

def test_pipeline_basic():
    pipeline = NLPPipeline("es")
    result = pipeline.process_to_dict("Hola mundo. ¿Cómo estás?")
    # assertions...
```

### Requirements
- Test should complete in <5 seconds
- Test should be deterministic
- Test should not require external files (use inline text)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file exists at `priv/python/tests/test_pipeline.py`
- [ ] Running `pytest` passes
- [ ] All assertions verify correct structure and values
- [ ] Test runs in under 5 seconds

## Testing
This IS the test task - no additional tests needed

3. Asserts at least 2 sentences are returned
4. Checks all required sentence fields: position, text, char_start, char_end, tokens
5. Validates 1-indexed sequential sentence positions
6. Checks all required token fields: position, surface, normalized_surface, lemma, pos, is_punctuation, char_start, char_end
7. Validates 1-indexed sequential token positions within sentences

specification:
## Goal
Add tests verifying that navigation keybinds dismiss the LLM popup.

## Test File
`test/lex_web/live/reader_live/show_test.exs`

## Test Cases

### Test 1: j key dismisses LLM popup and navigates next
```elixir
test "j key dismisses open LLM popup and navigates to next sentence", %{conn: conn, document: document} do
  {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/read/#{document.id}")
  
  # First open the popup with space on a word
  view
  |> element("[data-selectable='true']", _)  # a selectable token
  |> render_click()
  
  # Press space to open popup
  render_keydown(view, "space")
  
  # Assert popup is visible
  assert has_element?(view, "[data-testid='llm-popup']")
  
  # Press j to navigate
  render_keydown(view, "j")
  
  # Assert popup is dismissed
  refute has_element?(view, "[data-testid='llm-popup']")
  
  # Assert navigated (check for new sentence content or other indicator)
end
```

### Test 2: k key dismisses LLM popup
Similar to above but with "k" key and previous sentence.

### Test 3: w key dismisses LLM popup
Test that "w" (next token) dismisses popup.

### Test 4: b key dismisses LLM popup  
Test that "b" (previous token) dismisses popup.

### Test 5: Navigation still works when popup is closed
```elixir
test "navigation works normally when popup is not open", %{conn: conn, document: document} do
  {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/read/#{document.id}")
  
  # Press j without opening popup
  render_keydown(view, "j")
  
  # Assert navigated successfully
  # (check sentence changed, etc.)
end
```

## Helper Functions Needed
You may need to add or use existing test helpers:
- `render_keydown(view, key)` - simulate key press
- `has_element?(view, selector)` - check element exists

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test for j key dismissing popup passes
- [ ] Test for k key dismissing popup passes
- [ ] Test for w key dismissing popup passes
- [ ] Test for b key dismissing popup passes
- [ ] Test for navigation without popup passes

specification:
## Goal
Add comprehensive tests for the space key behavior that toggles word learning state.

## Test File
`test/lex_web/live/reader_live/show_test.exs`

## Test Cases

### Test 1: Space on closed popup sets word to learning and opens popup
```elixir
test "space when popup closed sets word to learning and opens popup", %{conn: conn, document: document} do
  {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/read/#{document.id}")
  
  # Focus a token first
  view
  |> element("[data-selectable='true']", _)  # a selectable token
  |> render_click()
  
  # Verify popup is not visible
  refute has_element?(view, "[data-testid='llm-popup']")
  
  # Press space
  render_keydown(view, "space")
  
  # Assert popup is now visible
  assert has_element?(view, "[data-testid='llm-popup']")
  
  # Assert word is now learning (check for learning styling or state)
  # The token should have learning status class
end
```

### Test 2: Space on open popup sets word to known and closes popup
```elixir
test "space when popup open sets word to known and closes popup", %{conn: conn, document: document} do
  {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/read/#{document.id}")
  
  # Focus a token and open popup
  view
  |> element("[data-selectable='true']", _)
  |> render_click()
  render_keydown(view, "space")
  
  # Verify popup is open and word is learning
  assert has_element?(view, "[data-testid='llm-popup']")
  
  # Press space again
  render_keydown(view, "space")
  
  # Assert popup is closed
  refute has_element?(view, "[data-testid='llm-popup']")
  
  # Assert word is now known (check for known styling - no background)
end
```

### Test 3: Space cycles through states correctly
```elixir
test "space cycles word through learning and known states correctly", %{conn: conn, document: document} do
  {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/read/#{document.id}")
  
  # Focus token
  view
  |> element("[data-selectable='true']", _)
  |> render_click()
  
  # Initial state: new/seen
  # After first space: learning + popup open
  render_keydown(view, "space")
  assert has_element?(view, "[data-testid='llm-popup']")
  # verify learning styling
  
  # After second space: known + popup closed
  render_keydown(view, "space")
  refute has_element?(view, "[data-testid='llm-popup']")
  # verify known styling
  
  # After third space: should be learning again + popup open
  render_keydown(view, "space")
  assert has_element?(view, "[data-testid='llm-popup']")
  # verify learning styling
end
```

### Test 4: Space without focused token handles gracefully
```elixir
test "space without focused token handles gracefully", %{conn: conn, document: document} do
  {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/read/#{document.id}")
  
  # Don't focus any token (focused_token_index should be 0)
  # Press space
  render_keydown(view, "space")
  
  # Should not crash
  # May open sentence-level help or do nothing
  # Verify view is still functional
end
```

## Test Setup Requirements
- Document with sentences and tokens
- User with reading position in the document
- May need to mock LLM calls or handle async responses

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test for space setting learning + opening popup passes
- [ ] Test for space setting known + closing popup passes
- [ ] Test for space cycling through states passes
- [ ] Test for space without focused token passes
- [ ] Tests verify both popup state AND word styling/status
- [ ] All tests run with `mix test test/lex_web/live/reader_live/show_test.exs`
completion_summary:
Successfully implemented comprehensive tests for space key word status toggle behavior in test/lex_web/live/reader_live/show_test.exs:

1. **Test 1**: "space when popup closed sets word to learning and opens popup" - Verifies that pressing space with a focused token sets it to learning status and opens the LLM help popup

2. **Test 2**: "space when popup open sets word to known and closes popup" - Verifies that pressing space again when popup is open sets the word to known status and closes the popup

3. **Test 3**: "space cycles word through learning and known states correctly" - Verifies the complete cycle: space press 1 (learning + open), space press 2 (known + close), space press 3 (learning + open)

4. **Test 4**: "space without focused token handles gracefully" - Verifies the view doesn't crash and shows sentence-level help placeholder when space is pressed without a focused token

All 27 tests pass, and code quality checks (formatting, credo) pass without issues in the modified file.

name: Build JavaScript streaming hook
description: Create JavaScript hook for receiving LLM stream chunks and updating popup content
specification:
## Task: Build JavaScript Streaming Hook

Create/update JavaScript to handle LLM streaming events from LiveView.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Hook Structure**
   - Create new file: `assets/js/hooks/llm_popup.js`
   - Export as `LlmPopup` hook
   - Or extend existing KeyboardNav hook if simpler

2. **Event Handling**
   - Listen for `llm_chunk` events from LiveView
   - Listen for `llm_done` events
   - Listen for `llm_error` events
   - Use `this.handleEvent(event, callback)` pattern

3. **DOM Updates**
   - Find `.llm-popup-content` element
   - On `llm_chunk`: append content to element, scroll to bottom
   - On `llm_done`: remove loading indicator if present
   - On `llm_error`: show error message

4. **Auto-scroll Behavior**
   - Auto-scroll content to bottom as new text arrives
   - Only if user hasn't manually scrolled up recently (within last 2 seconds)
   - Track last manual scroll timestamp

5. **Content Sanitization**
   - Use `textContent` or safely escape HTML to prevent XSS
   - Support basic markdown rendering (optional - can be plain text for MVP)

6. **Integration with KeyboardNav**
   - Ensure space key handling works correctly when popup is visible
   - May need coordination between hooks or use single hook
   - Consider merging into KeyboardNav since it already handles space

## Files to Create/Modify
- `assets/js/hooks/llm_popup.js` (new)
- `assets/js/app.js` (register hook)
- `assets/js/hooks/keyboard_nav.js` (may need coordination)

## Testing
- Manual browser testing
- Verify streaming chunks appear smoothly
- Verify space key dismisses popup
completion_summary:
Created JavaScript hook for receiving LLM stream chunks and updating popup content.

**Created Files:**
- `assets/js/hooks/llm_popup.js` - New hook handling LLM streaming events

**Modified Files:**
- `assets/js/app.js` - Registered LLMPopup hook
- `assets/js/hooks/keyboard_nav.js` - Added coordination to skip space key when popup visible

**Implementation Details:**

1. **Event Handling:**
   - Listens for `llm_chunk` events - appends content to response element using `document.createTextNode()` for XSS protection
   - Listens for `llm_done` events - hides loading indicator
   - Listens for `llm_error` events - displays error message
   - Uses `this.handleEvent(event, callback)` pattern from LiveView

2. **Auto-scroll Behavior:**
   - Auto-scrolls content to bottom as new text arrives
   - Tracks last manual scroll timestamp
   - Only auto-scrolls if user hasn't scrolled manually within last 2 seconds
   - Uses `isScrolledToBottom()` check with 10px threshold

3. **Space Key Handling:**
   - LLMPopup hook captures space key when popup is visible
   - Pushes `dismiss_llm_popup` event to LiveView
   - KeyboardNav hook checks popup visibility and skips space handling when visible
   - Uses capture phase listener to intercept before KeyboardNav

4. **DOM Updates:**
   - Finds `.llm-popup-content`, `.llm-popup-response`, `.llm-popup-loading`, `.llm-popup-error` elements
   - Updates elements in `mounted()` and `updated()` callbacks
   - Properly cleans up event listeners in `destroyed()`

**Quality Checks:**
- Elixir tests pass (301 tests, 1 pre-existing failure)
- Code formatted with `mix format`
- Credo passes with no new warnings
- All changes committed to git

name: Build LLM streaming client module
description: Create generic OpenAI-compatible streaming HTTP client for LLM chat completions
specification:
## Task: Build LLM Streaming Client Module

Create `Lex.LLM.Client` module for streaming chat completions via HTTP.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Module Structure**
   - Create `lib/lex/llm/client.ex`
   - Define `Lex.LLM.Client` module

2. **Stream Function**
   - `stream_chat_completion(messages, callback)` function
   - Takes list of message maps (%{role: "user" | "system", content: string})
   - Takes callback function that receives `{:chunk, content}` or `{:done, response_stats}` or `{:error, reason}
   - Returns `{:ok, Task.t()}` immediately (starts async task)

3. **HTTP Implementation**
   - Use `Req` library (already in deps) for HTTP streaming
   - POST to `#{base_url}/chat/completions`
   - Headers: `Authorization: Bearer #{api_key}`, `Content-Type: application/json`
   - Body includes: model, messages, stream: true
   - Parse SSE (Server-Sent Events) format from response

4. **Response Parsing**
   - Parse OpenAI streaming format: `data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":"..."}}]}`
   - Accumulate content deltas and call callback with each chunk
   - On `data: [DONE]`, call callback with full usage stats (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens)

5. **Error Handling**
   - Handle HTTP errors (4xx, 5xx) → `{:error, :http_error, status, body}`
   - Handle network errors → `{:error, :network_error, reason}`
   - Handle timeout → `{:error, :timeout}`
   - Handle invalid JSON → `{:error, :invalid_response, raw}`

6. **Configuration Integration**
   - Read config from Application.get_env(:lex, :llm_*)
   - Handle nil API key gracefully (return `{:error, :not_configured}`)

7. **Types**
   - Define `@type message :: %{role: String.t(), content: String.t()}`
   - Define `@type chunk_callback :: ({:chunk, String.t()} | {:done, map()} | {:error, term()} -> any())`

## Files to Create
- `lib/lex/llm/client.ex`

## Testing
- Unit tests for request building
- Unit tests for SSE parsing
- Mock tests for happy path and error cases
- Tests require mocking Req
completion_summary:
Successfully implemented Lex.LLM.Client module for streaming LLM chat completions:

**Created files:**
- `lib/lex/llm/client.ex` - Main client module with streaming HTTP support
- `test/lex/llm/client_test.exs` - Test suite with 10 passing tests
- Updated `config/test.exs` with test LLM configuration

**Features implemented:**
- `stream_chat_completion/2` - Returns `{:ok, Task.t()}` and streams via callback
- Uses Mint for HTTP streaming
- Parses OpenAI-compatible SSE format (data: {...})
- Callback receives: `{:chunk, content}`, `{:done, stats}`, or `{:error, reason}`
- Configuration via Application env (:llm_api_key, :llm_base_url, :llm_model, :llm_timeout_ms)
- Error handling: :not_configured, :http_error, :network_error, :timeout
- Token estimation for usage stats

**Quality checks:**
- All 10 tests pass
- Mix format clean
- Credo clean (no issues in new code)

name: Build reading events logging system
description: Log all reader interactions to reading_events table
specification:
## Goal
Create infrastructure to log all reader actions for analytics.

## Event Types
```elixir
:enter_sentence    - User views a sentence (on mount/navigate)
:advance_sentence  - User presses j to advance
:skip_range        - User skips section
:mark_learning     - User marks word learning
:unmark_learning   - User unmarks learning
:llm_help_requested - User presses space for help
```

## Implementation
```elixir
Lex.Reader.log_event(user_id, event_type, metadata \\ %{})
# => {:ok, reading_event} | {:error, changeset}

# Example metadata:
# :advance_sentence -> %{document_id: 1, from_sentence_id: 5, to_sentence_id: 6}
# :mark_learning -> %{document_id: 1, sentence_id: 5, token_id: 12, lexeme_id: 34}
```

## Design Decisions
- Fire-and-forget: don't block navigation on logging failure
- Log in same transaction as state changes when possible
- JSON payload for flexible metadata
- No updated_at (per schema)

## Tests Required
- All event types can be logged
- Metadata properly serialized to JSON
- Failed log doesn't break navigation
- Events have correct user_id and timestamps

## Files
- Add to `lib/lex/reader.ex`
- Tests in `test/lex/reader_test.exs`
completion_summary:
Successfully implemented the reading events logging system:

1. Added `log_event/3` function to `Lex.Reader` module at lib/lex/reader.ex:200-245
   - Accepts user_id, event_type (atom), and metadata map
   - Converts event type atoms to strings for database storage
   - Serializes metadata to JSON payload using ReadingEvent.encode_payload/1
   - Extracts optional foreign keys (document_id, sentence_id, token_id) from metadata
   - Returns {:ok, ReadingEvent} or {:error, changeset}

2. Added comprehensive tests in test/lex/reader_test.exs covering:
   - All 6 event types: enter_sentence, advance_sentence, skip_range, mark_learning, unmark_learning, llm_help_requested
   - Metadata JSON serialization with nested structures
   - Correct user_id and timestamp assignment
   - Error handling for invalid event types and missing required fields
   - Empty metadata handling
   - Foreign key extraction from metadata

All 24 reader tests pass. Code is properly formatted with no compilation warnings.

- Add UI to `reader_live/show.html.heex`
- Add tests

description: Implement EPUB metadata extraction function
specification:
## Task
Implement `Lex.Library.EPUB.parse_metadata/1` that extracts document metadata from EPUB.

## Implementation Details
- Use `paper_street` to open EPUB and access OPF metadata
- Extract these fields from Dublin Core metadata:
  - `:title` from `dc:title` (first if multiple)
  - `:author` from `dc:creator` (first if multiple)
  - `:language` from `dc:language` (default to "es" if missing)
- Return `{:ok, %{title: string, author: string, language: string}}` or `{:error, reason}`

## Error Cases to Handle
- File not found → `{:error, :file_not_found}`
- Not a valid ZIP/EPUB → `{:error, :invalid_epub}`
- Missing OPF → `{:error, :missing_opf}`
- Missing title → use filename as fallback with warning log

## File
`lib/lex/library/epub.ex`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Successfully parses El Principito metadata
- [ ] Returns correct title, author, language
- [ ] Handles missing metadata gracefully (fallbacks/warnings)
- [ ] Returns error tuples for bad inputs
- [ ] Unit tests with test fixture

name: Create NLP module with process_text function
description: Implement Python NLP coordination module
specification:
## Task
Create `Lex.Text.NLP` module that interfaces with `priv/python/lex_nlp.py`.

## Implementation

### `process_text(text, opts \\ [])`
Main function that:
1. Creates temp directory using `System.tmp_dir()`
2. Writes text to temp input file (UTF-8)
3. Generates temp output file path
4. Builds command: `python priv/python/lex_nlp.py --input <input> --output <output>`
5. Executes with `System.cmd/3` (timeout: 30s)
6. Reads and parses JSON output
7. Cleans up temp files in `ensure` block
8. Returns `{:ok, sentences_list}` or `{:error, reason}`

## Return Format
```elixir
{:ok, [
  %{
    position: 1,
    text: "...",
    char_start: 0,
    char_end: 18,
    tokens: [
      %{position: 1, surface: "...", normalized_surface: "...", lemma: "...", pos: "DET", is_punctuation: false, char_start: 0, char_end: 2},
      ...
    ]
  },
  ...
]}
```

## Error Handling
- Python not in PATH → `{:error, :python_not_found}`
- Python exits non-zero → `{:error, {:python_exit, code, stderr}}`
- Invalid JSON → `{:error, {:invalid_json, reason}}`
- Timeout → `{:error, :timeout}`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Successfully processes Spanish text through Python
- [ ] Returns correctly structured sentence/token data
- [ ] Cleans up temp files even on failure
- [ ] All error cases handled with descriptive tuples
- [ ] Unit tests with mocked System.cmd

name: Create Reader context with position management
description: Build context module for reading position CRUD operations
specification:
## Goal
Create `Lex.Reader` context with functions to get and set reading positions.

## Functions to Implement

```elixir
# Get or create position for user/document
Lex.Reader.get_or_create_position(user_id, document_id)
# => {:ok, %ReadingPosition{}}

# Update position (sentence-level only for now)
Lex.Reader.set_position(user_id, document_id, section_id, sentence_id)
# => {:ok, %ReadingPosition{}}
```

## Behavior
- `get_or_create_position`: Returns existing position or creates new one at first sentence of first section
- `set_position`: Updates existing position record
- Positions are sentence-level (no token_position persistence per requirements)

## Schema
Use existing `reading_positions` table:
- user_id, document_id, section_id, sentence_id
- active_token_position can be null/ignored for now

## Tests Required
- Get position returns existing
- Get position creates new at document start if none exists
- Set position updates correctly
- Returns error for invalid document_id

## Files

name: Create import_epub orchestration function
description: Build end-to-end import function that coordinates all steps
specification:
## Task
Create `Lex.Library.import_epub/2` that orchestrates complete EPUB import pipeline.

## Implementation

### Function Signature
```elixir
import_epub(file_path, opts \\ [])
# opts:
#   :user_id (required) - User to associate document with
#   :source_file - Override source path in Document (default: file_path)
```

### Pipeline Steps (in Repo.transaction)
1. **Parse EPUB metadata**
   - Call `EPUB.parse_metadata/1`
   - Create Document with status "uploaded"

2. **List and filter chapters**
   - Call `EPUB.list_chapters/1`

3. **Process each chapter**
   - Get chapter text: `EPUB.get_chapter_content/2`
   - Create Section record
   - Call `NLP.process_text/1` on chapter text
   - For each sentence:
     - Create Sentence record
     - For each token:
       - Create/find Lexeme (by language, normalized_lemma, pos)
       - Create Token record linked to Sentence and Lexeme

4. **Finalize**
   - Update Document status to "ready"
   - Return `{:ok, document}`

### Transaction Safety
- Wrap ALL database operations in `Repo.transaction/1`
- Any error rolls back everything
- Return `{:error, reason}` from transaction

## Error Handling
- EPUB parse error → `{:error, {:epub_parse_failed, reason}}`
- NLP error → `{:error, {:nlp_failed, section_title, reason}}`
- Validation error → `{:error, {:validation_failed, changeset}}`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Successfully imports El Principito complete book
- [ ] Creates Document with correct metadata
- [ ] Creates all Sections in order
- [ ] Creates Sentences and Tokens with correct positions
- [ ] Links Tokens to Lexemes
- [ ] Transaction rolls back on any failure
- [ ] Integration test with test fixture

- Complete pipeline coordination: EPUB parsing → Document creation → Section creation → NLP processing → Sentence/Token creation → Lexeme linking → Document finalization
- All operations wrapped in Repo.transaction for transactional safety
- Proper error handling with three error tuple types: epub_parse_failed, nlp_failed, validation_failed

description: Make navigation keys dismiss the LLM popup before navigating
specification:
## Goal
Ensure all navigation keybinds dismiss any open LLM popup before performing navigation.

## Implementation Details

### File Changes
- **LiveView**: `lib/lex_web/live/reader_live/show.ex`

### Logic Changes
In the `handle_event("key_nav", ...)` function, modify each navigation handler to:
1. First check if `llm_popup_visible` is true
2. If true, dismiss the popup (reuse `dismiss_llm_popup/1`)
3. Then perform the navigation action

### Affected Navigation Keys
- `j` - next sentence
- `k` - previous sentence  
- `w` - next token
- `b` - previous token
- Arrow keys (if already supported, check current implementation)

### Implementation Approach
Option 1: Modify each handler to check and dismiss:
```elixir
defp handle_next_sentence(socket) do
  socket = maybe_dismiss_popup(socket)
  # ... rest of navigation logic
end

 defp maybe_dismiss_popup(socket) do
   if socket.assigns.llm_popup_visible do
     dismiss_llm_popup(socket) |> elem(1)  # extract socket from {:noreply, socket}
   else
     socket
   end
 end
```

Option 2: Check once in `handle_event("key_nav", ...)` before dispatching to handlers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pressing `j`, `k`, `w`, or `b` dismisses open LLM popup
- [ ] Navigation still occurs after popup dismissal
- [ ] No errors if popup is already closed
- [ ] Existing `dismiss_llm_popup/1` function reused where possible

name: Document hierarchy migrations and schemas
description: Create documents, sections, and sentences tables with migrations and Ecto schemas
specification:
## Goal
Create the core document hierarchy tables for storing uploaded EPUBs and their content structure.

## Scope

### Table 1: `documents`
**Columns:**
- `id` (bigint, primary key)
- `user_id` (bigint, foreign key to users, required)
- `title` (string, required)
- `author` (string, nullable)
- `language` (string, required, BCP-47 code, MVP: "es")
- `status` (string, required, enum: "uploaded" | "processing" | "ready" | "failed")
- `source_file` (string, required, path to stored EPUB)
- `inserted_at` (utc_datetime)
- `updated_at` (utc_datetime)

**Indexes:**
- Index on `user_id` (FK lookup, user's library)
- Index on `status` (filter by processing status)
- Index on `(user_id, status)` (common query: user's documents by status)
- Index on `language` (filter by language)

**Foreign Keys:**
- `user_id` references `users(id)` with `on_delete: :delete` (cascade delete documents when user is deleted)

### Table 2: `sections`
**Columns:**
- `id` (bigint, primary key)
- `document_id` (bigint, foreign key to documents, required)
- `position` (integer, required, 1..N within document)
- `title` (string, nullable, chapter title)
- `source_href` (string, nullable, EPUB reference)
- `inserted_at` (utc_datetime)
- `updated_at` (utc_datetime)

**Indexes:**
- Index on `document_id` (FK lookup)
- Unique index on `(document_id, position)` (enforce ordering within document)
- Index on `(document_id, position)` for ordered retrieval

**Foreign Keys:**
- `document_id` references `documents(id)` with `on_delete: :delete` (cascade delete sections when document is deleted)

### Table 3: `sentences`
**Columns:**
- `id` (bigint, primary key)
- `section_id` (bigint, foreign key to sections, required)
- `position` (integer, required, 1..N within section)
- `text` (text, required, the sentence text)
- `char_start` (integer, nullable, character offset in section)
- `char_end` (integer, nullable, character offset in section)
- `inserted_at` (utc_datetime)
- `updated_at` (utc_datetime)

**Indexes:**
- Index on `section_id` (FK lookup)
- Unique index on `(section_id, position)` (enforce ordering within section)
- Index on `(section_id, position)` for ordered retrieval

**Foreign Keys:**
- `section_id` references `sections(id)` with `on_delete: :delete` (cascade delete sentences when section is deleted)

### Migration Files
Create separate migration files:
- `priv/repo/migrations/003_create_documents.exs`
- `priv/repo/migrations/004_create_sections.exs`
- `priv/repo/migrations/005_create_sentences.exs`

### Ecto Schemas
Create schema modules:
- `lib/lex/library/document.ex` - Document schema
- `lib/lex/library/section.ex` - Section schema
- `lib/lex/text/sentence.ex` - Sentence schema

**Schema requirements:**
- Define all fields with correct types
- Define `belongs_to` / `has_many` relationships:
  - Document belongs_to user, has_many sections
  - Section belongs_to document, has_many sentences
  - Sentence belongs_to section
- Include `timestamps()` macro
- Add changeset functions for validation:
  - Document: validate required fields, status enum values
  - Section: validate required fields, position > 0
  - Sentence: validate required fields, position > 0

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Migrations run successfully with `mix ecto.migrate`
- [ ] Unique constraints on (document_id, position) and (section_id, position) enforced
- [ ] Foreign key constraints enforced with cascade delete
- [ ] Cascade delete works correctly (deleting document removes sections and sentences)
- [ ] Schema modules compile without errors
- [ ] Changesets validate required fields and enums
- [ ] Indexes created for all foreign keys and query patterns
- [ ] Document status field restricted to valid enum values

## Testing
- Run `mix ecto.migrate` to verify migrations
- Run `mix compile` to verify schemas compile
- Test cascade delete behavior in iex session
- Test unique constraint violations in iex session
completion_summary:
Created migrations and Ecto schemas for document hierarchy tables:

**Migrations:**
- `003_create_documents.exs` - documents table with user_id FK (cascade delete), title, author, language, status, source_file, and indexes on user_id, status, (user_id, status), language
- `004_create_sections.exs` - sections table with document_id FK (cascade delete), position, title, source_href, unique index on (document_id, position)
- `005_create_sentences.exs` - sentences table with section_id FK (cascade delete), position, text, char_start, char_end, unique index on (section_id, position)

**Schemas:**
- `lib/lex/library/document.ex` - Document schema with belongs_to user, has_many sections, validates required fields and status enum (uploaded|processing|ready|failed)
- `lib/lex/library/section.ex` - Section schema with belongs_to document, has_many sentences, validates required fields and position >= 1
- `lib/lex/text/sentence.ex` - Sentence schema with belongs_to section, validates required fields and position >= 1

**Verified:**
- All migrations run successfully with `mix ecto.migrate`
- Unique constraints on (document_id, position) and (section_id, position) enforced
- Foreign key cascade delete works (tested - deleting document removes sections and sentences)
- All indexes created for FK lookups and query patterns
- Changesets validate required fields, status enum, and position >= 1

name: Fix space key word status toggle behavior
description: Change space key to toggle word: closed→learning+open, open→known+close
specification:
## Goal
Fix the space key behavior so it properly toggles word learning state in conjunction with the LLM popup.

## Current Behavior (Broken)
- Space (popup closed) → opens popup, sets word to learning
- Space (popup open) → closes popup, word stays learning
- Space again → opens popup, sets word to known

## Desired Behavior
- Space (popup closed) → sets word to learning AND opens LLM popup
- Space (popup open) → sets word to known AND closes LLM popup

## Implementation Details

### File Changes
- **LiveView**: `lib/lex_web/live/reader_live/show.ex`

### Current Code Location
`handle_llm_help/1` function (around line 462) and `handle_token_level_help/3`.

### Required Changes

1. **When popup is closed and space is pressed:**
   - Set word to learning state (use `Vocab.toggle_learning/2` or ensure `advance_help_state` goes to learning)
   - Open LLM popup with help request

2. **When popup is open and space is pressed:**
   - Set word to known state
   - Close LLM popup

### Implementation Approach
Modify `handle_llm_help/1`:

```elixir
defp handle_llm_help(socket) do
  if socket.assigns.llm_popup_visible do
    # Popup is open: set to known and close
    socket = set_word_to_known(socket)  # implement this
    dismiss_llm_popup(socket)
  else
    # Popup is closed: set to learning and open
    socket = set_word_to_learning(socket)  # implement this
    start_llm_help_request(socket)
  end
end
```

You'll need to:
1. Extract the word state setting logic from `handle_token_level_help` or create new helper functions
2. Ensure the word status is updated BEFORE opening/closing popup
3. Update `lexeme_states` assign after status change

### State Tracking
The word status is tracked in `socket.assigns.lexeme_states` (a map of lexeme_id -> state).

## Edge Cases
- No focused token (index 0): should handle gracefully
- Token is punctuation: should handle gracefully
- Token has no lexeme_id: should handle gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Space (popup closed) sets word to learning AND opens popup
- [ ] Space (popup open) sets word to known AND closes popup
- [ ] Counter display (R/L/K) updates after each space press
- [ ] Token styling updates to reflect status change (blue for learning, no highlight for known)
- [ ] Edge cases handled gracefully (no crashes)
completion_summary:
Successfully implemented the space key word status toggle behavior:

**Changes Made:**
1. Modified `handle_llm_help/1` to manage word state before opening/closing popup:
   - When popup is closed: sets word to learning (if focused) and opens popup
   - When popup is open: sets word to known (if focused) and closes popup

2. Added helper functions:
   - `set_focused_word_to_learning/1`: Sets focused word to learning state
   - `set_focused_word_to_known/1`: Sets focused word to known state
   - `mark_known/2`: Creates or updates UserLexemeState to known status

3. Updated `handle_token_level_help/5` to remove state management logic (now handled in `handle_llm_help`)

4. Updated tests in `state_transitions_test.exs` to match new behavior:
   - Test for "space (popup closed) sets word to learning and opens popup"
   - Test for "space (popup open) sets word to known and closes popup"

**Quality Checks:**
- All 319 tests pass

name: Implement LLM help request handling
description: Handle spacebar to request help and mark token as learning
specification:
## Goal
Handle LLM help requests and auto-mark words as learning.

## Behavior (per user clarification)
When user presses `space`:
1. If focused token: request word explanation, mark as `learning`
2. If no focused token: request sentence explanation
3. Log the request in `llm_help_requests` table
4. Log reading event `:llm_help_requested`

## Implementation
```elixir
# In ReaderLive.Show
handle_event("llm_help", %{"token_id" => token_id}, socket) do
  # Mark as learning
  {:ok, _} = Lex.Vocab.mark_learning(user_id, token_id)
  
  # Log request
  {:ok, _} = Lex.Vocab.log_llm_request(user_id, document_id, sentence_id, token_id, :token)
  
  # Log event
  {:ok, _} = Lex.Reader.log_event(user_id, :llm_help_requested, %{token_id: token_id, ...})
  
  # Placeholder for actual LLM call (M5)
  {:noreply, assign(socket, help_requested: true)}
end
```

## Functions to Add
```elixir
Lex.Vocab.mark_learning(user_id, token_id)
# Directly set to learning (not toggle)

Lex.Vocab.log_llm_request(user_id, document_id, sentence_id, token_id, request_type)
# Creates llm_help_requests row with provider/model from config
```

## UI
- Show "Help requested" indicator
- Display will be implemented in M5

## Tests Required
- Space with focused token marks learning
- Space without focused token requests sentence help
- LLM request logged with correct IDs
- Reading event logged

## Files

- Modify `reader_live/show.ex`
- Tests

3. Spacebar handling in `reader_live/show.ex` (line 110-116, 119-191)

   - If focused token: marks as learning, logs token-level request, logs reading event
   - If no focused token: logs sentence-level request, logs reading event

name: Implement chapter listing
description: Extract ordered chapter list from EPUB spine
specification:
## Task
Implement `Lex.Library.EPUB.list_chapters/1` that returns ordered chapters excluding front/back matter.

## Implementation Details
- Parse OPF file to get manifest and spine
- Follow spine order (that's the reading order)
- Filter out items marked `linear="no"` (front/back matter)
- For each chapter item, extract:
  - `:id` - manifest item id
  - `:title` - from nav/toc if available, else derive from href
  - `:href` - relative path to content file
  - `:position` - 1-based index in reading order

## Return
`{:ok, [%{id: string, title: string, href: string, position: integer}]}`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns El Principito chapters in correct reading order
- [ ] Excludes front matter (copyright, dedication, etc.)
- [ ] Position field is 1-based sequential
- [ ] Unit tests

- Parses OPF file to get manifest items and spine order

- Maps spine items to manifest items using idref → id lookup
- Extracts: id, title (from item description or derived from href), href, position (1-based)
- Title derivation handles various filename patterns (underscores, dashes, letter/number boundaries)

name: Implement first encounter detection
description: Mark unseen lexemes as seen when sentence is first displayed
specification:
## Goal
Implement logic to detect when a user first encounters a sentence and mark all non-punctuation lexemes as `seen`.

## Behavior
When a sentence is displayed (mounted or navigated to):
1. Get all non-punctuation tokens in the sentence
2. For each token's lexeme, check if user_lexeme_state exists
3. For lexemes with no state, create `user_lexeme_state` with status=`seen`
4. Increment `seen_count` for existing `seen` entries
5. Update `last_seen_at` timestamp

## Implementation
```elixir
Lex.Vocab.mark_lexemes_seen(user_id, sentence_id)
# => {:ok, [user_lexeme_state]} | {:error, changeset}
```

## Edge Cases
- Repeated tokens in one sentence: only update once per lexeme
- Punctuation tokens: ignored completely
- Already `learning` or `known`: do nothing (preserve higher states)

## Tests Required
- First view creates seen entries for all non-punct tokens
- Second view of same sentence doesn't duplicate
- Punctuation ignored
- Learning/known words not affected
- Repeated tokens in sentence handled correctly

## Files
- Add to `lib/lex/vocab.ex`
- Tests in `test/lex/vocab_test.exs`
completion_summary:
Successfully implemented `Lex.Vocab.mark_lexemes_seen/2` that marks all non-punctuation lexemes in a sentence as seen for a user.

**Implementation:**
- Added `mark_lexemes_seen/2` function to `lib/lex/vocab.ex` that:
  - Gets all non-punctuation tokens for a sentence with distinct lexeme IDs
  - Processes each lexeme within a database transaction
  - Creates new `user_lexeme_state` with status="seen" for unseen lexemes
  - Increments `seen_count` and updates `last_seen_at` for existing "seen" entries
  - Preserves "learning" and "known" states (returns them unchanged)
  - Ignores punctuation tokens and tokens without lexeme_id
  - Handles repeated tokens correctly by using `distinct` in the query

**Testing:**
- Created comprehensive tests in `test/lex/vocab_test.exs` covering:
  - First view creates seen entries for all non-punctuation tokens
  - Second view increments seen_count without creating duplicates
  - Punctuation tokens are ignored completely
  - Learning and known words are not affected
  - Repeated tokens in sentence are handled correctly (only one update)
  - Tokens without lexeme_id are ignored
  - Multiple users can have separate states for the same lexeme
  - Empty sentences return empty list
  - Sentences with only punctuation return empty list

All 10 vocab tests pass successfully.

name: Implement learning toggle

## Goal

## Behavior

1. Get lexeme for focused token
2. Check current state:
   - If no state or `seen`: create/update to `learning`, set `learning_since`
   - If `learning`: revert to `seen` (not `known`)
   - If `known`: do nothing or show warning (decision: do nothing)

Lex.Vocab.toggle_learning(user_id, token_id)

- Keyboard shortcut: `l` (suggested) or `m`
- Visual: Learning words show blue box

name: Implement mark sentence as read
description: Mark sentence as read when advancing via normal navigation
specification:
## Goal
Implement sentence read state tracking.

## Behavior
When `advance_sentence` is triggered:
1. Create or update `user_sentence_state` for the sentence being left
2. Set status=`read` and `read_at` timestamp

## Implementation
```elixir
Lex.Reader.mark_sentence_read(user_id, sentence_id)
# => {:ok, user_sentence_state} | {:error, changeset}
```

## Rules
- Only happens on normal advance (j key)
- Does NOT happen on:
  - Skip section action
  - Going backward (k key)
  - Direct navigation (clicking document from list)

## Tests Required
- Mark sentence read on advance
- No change on skip
- No change on backward navigation
- Idempotent (multiple advances don't create duplicates)

## Files
- Add to `lib/lex/reader.ex`
- Tests in `test/lex/reader_test.exs`

Successfully implemented `Lex.Reader.mark_sentence_read/2` that marks sentences as read when advancing via normal navigation.

**Implementation:**
- Added `mark_sentence_read/2` function to `lib/lex/reader.ex` that:
  - Creates or updates `user_sentence_state` for the given user and sentence
  - Sets status to "read" and records the current timestamp in `read_at`
  - Is idempotent - multiple calls for the same user/sentence update the same record
  - Returns `{:ok, user_sentence_state}` on success or `{:error, changeset}` on failure

**Testing:**

  - Creating new sentence state with read status
  - Updating existing unread sentence state to read
  - Idempotency - multiple calls don't create duplicates

**Note:** The navigation rules (only on j key, not on skip/k key/direct navigation) are enforced at the LiveView level, not in this function. This function handles the data layer operation only.
